chunks.text: During a ceremony recognizing the service and contributions of outgoing leadership, EE Division Chair Clark Nguyen was commemorated with a bobblehead in his likeness, which also showcases one of his hobbies and his favored tool of instruction in EE electronics courses: the electric guitar.
chunks.question: What was Clark Nguyen commemorated with at the leadership ceremony?
chunks.answer: A bobblehead of himself with an electric guitar.

chunks.text: The 2024 Distinguished EECS Alumni Awards honored Nancy Amato, David Allstot, Richard Muray, Lars Rasmussen, and Kishore Seendripu at a private ceremony at the Faculty Club on May 7th, 2024.
chunks.question: Who were honored at the 2024 Distinguished EECS Alumni Awards?
chunks.answer: Nancy Amato, David Allstot, Richard Muray, Lars Rasmussen, Kishore Seendripu.

chunks.text: Each year the EECS Student Awards Committee selects winners for each of our department awards, many based on nominations gathered from EECS students, faculty and staff.
chunks.question: How are EECS department award winners selected?
chunks.answer: By the Student Awards Committee from nominations.

chunks.text: We invite you to recognize the profound contributions of Professor Joseph Thomas Gier to the University of California, the first tenured Black professor in the UC system. Sculptor Dana King unveiled a bronze statue in honor of Gier in the Blum Hall courtyard.
chunks.question: What was unveiled in honor of Professor Joseph Thomas Gier?
chunks.answer: A bronze statue by sculptor Dana King.

chunks.text: Although renowned roboticist Ruzena Bajcsy recently retired from Berkeley, she returned to discuss her insights on how robotics research has evolved over the past half-century with five senior colleagues who have combined research experience of over 200 years.
chunks.question: What did Ruzena Bajcsy discuss upon returning to Berkeley?
chunks.answer: The evolution of robotics research over 50 years.
